\documentclass{article}
\begin{document}
\section{Production network}
Let $\Omega$ use the user-by-supplier convention. Suppose $\rho(\Omega)<1$,
so $I-\Omega$ is invertible and the Neumann series converges.
\begin{equation}
  \mathcal L=(I-\Omega)^{-1}=I+\Omega+\Omega^2+\cdots
  \label{eq:leontief}
\end{equation}
Here $I$ is the identity matrix and matrix powers collect upstream paths.
\end{document}
